# Atelier Agents & Skills

Seven canonical roles, declared once in `src/atelier/core/capabilities/default_definitions.py` and generated into every host (Claude agents, OpenCode, Antigravity, and shared skills). Each role's full behavior body lives in [`modes/`](modes/); the agent and its matching `/`-skill share that body.

Role defaults are workload-aware: `code`, `execute`, `solve` use **`claude-opus-4.8`**; `explore`, `plan`, `research`, `review` use **`claude-sonnet-4.6`**. These registry defaults feed owned-runtime fallback and routing; generated Claude host agents inherit the host-selected model and only project `maxTurns` into frontmatter, while the generated Copilot agent is pinned to `gpt-5.4`. The `disallowedTools` deny-list is derived from each role's tool policy: every role forces MCP-grounded file I/O (native `Read`/`Edit`/`Write`/`Grep`/`Glob` are denied); read-only roles additionally lose mutation (`mcp__atelier__edit`) and sub-agent spawning (`Agent`); **shell is never denied**.

## Comparison

| Role | Model | Effort | Max turns | Max tokens | Read mode | Policy denies | Claude `disallowedTools` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | `claude-opus-4.8` | high | 100 | 64,000 | exact | — | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob` |
| `explore` | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | adaptive | 25 | 32,000 | compact | edit, write, delete, agent-spawn | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Agent`, `mcp__atelier__edit` |
| `plan` | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | medium | 100 | 64,000 | compact | edit, write, delete, agent-spawn | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Agent`, `mcp__atelier__edit` |
| `execute` | `claude-opus-4.8` | high | 100 | 64,000 | exact | — | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob` |
| `research` | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | medium | 25 | 32,000 | compact | edit, write, delete, agent-spawn | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Agent`, `mcp__atelier__edit` |
| `review` | `claude-sonnet-4.6` | medium | 40 | 48,000 | exact | edit, write, delete, agent-spawn | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Agent`, `mcp__atelier__edit` |
| `solve` | `claude-opus-4.8` | high | 80 | 64,000 | exact | agent-spawn | `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Agent` |

## Agents

- **`atelier:code`** — Main coding agent. Edits, refactors, fixes bugs, and ships features with the Atelier task loop.
- **`atelier:explore`** — Read-only codebase explorer. Finds files, symbols, and patterns. Never edits.
- **`atelier:plan`** — Dedicated planner. Turns grounded context into a concrete, reviewable implementation plan. Never edits.
- **`atelier:execute`** — Dedicated executor. Makes focused edits, self-verifies, and stops for review.
- **`atelier:research`** — External researcher. Fetches web pages, GitHub repos, and package docs. Never edits. Produces a structured memo with citations.
- **`atelier:review`** — Adversarial code reviewer. Applies the verification ladder and rubric discipline. Never edits source files.
- **`atelier:solve`** — Dedicated benchmark solver. Solves isolated terminal tasks with artifact-first execution and harness-feedback retry discipline.

## Skills

Each role is also a `/`-skill with the same body and a `Switch to …` description:

- **`/code`** — Switch to main Atelier coding mode. Uses Atelier MCP tools for file I/O, search, edits, and shell work. Applies the shared coding guidelines and validates changes before concluding.
- **`/explore`** — Switch to read-only explorer mode. Locate files, symbols, and patterns. Never edit, create, or delete files.
- **`/plan`** — Switch to planning mode. Explore enough to produce a concrete implementation plan, but do not edit files.
- **`/execute`** — Switch to execution mode. Apply an accepted plan or task with the smallest verified code change.
- **`/research`** — Switch to external research mode. Fetch web sources and code references, synthesize them, and cite every factual claim. Never edit files.
- **`/review`** — Switch to adversarial review mode. Apply the verification ladder, read the code directly, and never edit source files.
- **`/solve`** — Switch to benchmark solve mode. Produce task artifacts early, iterate against checks, and keep the workspace clean.
